title: Alectorolophus songeonii
intro: Alectorolophus songeonii, also known as Mountain Stock, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, characterized by its hairy leaves and yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic effects. The bioactive constituents of Alectorolophus songeonii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as tinctures and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Alectorolophus songeonii belongs to the family Cactaceae and is classified within the subfamily Cactoideae. This classification places it among the cacti with areoles and stems covered in sharp, protective spines. It further belongs to the tribe Cacteae. The genus Alectorolophus is a part of the subtribe Cactinae. The classification of Alectorolophus songeonii reflects its unique adaptations for arid environments.
